title: Jaws
plot_clue: A coastal community is terrorized by a massive predator lurking just beneath the surface. A police chief who is afraid of water must team up with an oceanographer and a seasoned hunter to stop the attacks.
explain: Spielberg's 1975 blockbuster pioneered the summer tentpole and created the phrase 'you're gonna need a bigger boat'.

title: The Silence of the Lambs
plot_clue: A young FBI trainee is sent to interview a brilliant but imprisoned psychiatrist to gain insights into the psychology of a serial killer who skins his victims.
explain: One of only three films to win all five major Academy Awards including Best Picture, Director, Actor, Actress, and Screenplay.

title: Back to the Future
plot_clue: A teenager is accidentally sent 30 years into the past in a time-traveling vehicle built by his eccentric scientist friend, and must make his parents fall in love to ensure his own existence.
explain: The DeLorean's flux capacitor needed 1.21 gigawatts to reach 88 mph — the exact speed required for time travel.

title: The Shining
plot_clue: A family spends the winter as caretakers of an isolated mountain hotel while the father, a struggling writer, descends into madness and turns violent toward his wife and psychic son.
explain: Stanley Kubrick shot Shelley Duvall's scenes repeatedly until she was genuinely terrified — her hair began falling out from stress.

title: Pulp Fiction
plot_clue: Several interconnected stories follow hitmen, a gangster's wife, a boxer, and a pair of robbers in the Los Angeles criminal underworld — told in a non-linear narrative.
explain: Tarantino's Palme d'Or winner revived John Travolta's career and popularized non-linear storytelling in mainstream cinema.

title: Forrest Gump
plot_clue: A man with a low IQ from Alabama unknowingly influences several major historical events in 20th-century America while pursuing the love of his life from his front porch bench.
explain: The visual effects team digitally inserted Tom Hanks into real historical footage — including a meeting with JFK.

title: The Matrix
plot_clue: A computer programmer discovers that reality as he knows it is a simulated construct created by machines, and joins a group of rebels fighting against their AI oppressors.
explain: The Wachowskis drew inspiration from Simulacra and Simulation by Jean Baudrillard — a copy of the book appears in the film.

title: Goodfellas
plot_clue: A young man rises through the ranks of the New York mob over several decades, drawn in by the glamour and power of organized crime before it all begins to unravel.
explain: The famous Copacabana steadicam shot — entering through the kitchen to a front-row table — took 30 takes to perfect.

title: Parasite
plot_clue: A poor family schemes their way into working for a wealthy household in Seoul, but their elaborate deception takes a dark and violent turn when an unexpected discovery is made.
explain: First non-English-language film to win the Oscars Best Picture — Bong Joon-ho quipped the award would 'cure the one-inch barrier of subtitles'.

title: Die Hard
plot_clue: An off-duty New York cop visits his estranged wife at her Los Angeles office Christmas party when the skyscraper is seized by a group of highly organized thieves posing as terrorists.
explain: The film's debate over whether it qualifies as a Christmas movie has generated more think-pieces than almost any movie of its era.

title: Alien
plot_clue: The crew of a deep-space mining vessel responds to a distress signal on a desolate moon and inadvertently brings back a lethal organism that systematically hunts them inside the ship.
explain: H.R. Giger designed the xenomorph — its biomechanical aesthetic gave the creature a uniquely unsettling sense of organic machinery.

title: Inception
plot_clue: A skilled thief who steals secrets from dreams is hired for the reverse task: planting an idea deep within a target's subconscious through multiple layers of dreaming.
explain: The rotating hallway fight sequence required building a 100-foot rotating set — Joseph Gordon-Levitt trained for weeks to perform it safely.

title: The Godfather
plot_clue: The patriarch of a powerful crime family is shot while refusing to support a rival gang's drug trade. His youngest son, initially uninvolved in crime, is drawn into the business to protect his family.
choices: Once Upon a Time in America
explain: Marlon Brando stuffed his cheeks with cotton wool for the audition — the final film used a specially fitted mouthpiece for the same effect.

title: Toy Story
plot_clue: A proud cowboy toy fears being replaced when his owner receives a flashy new space toy as a birthday gift, leading to a rivalry that becomes an unlikely friendship.
explain: The first fully computer-animated feature film — Pixar had just 110 employees when production began in 1993.

title: Get Out
plot_clue: A young Black man visits his white girlfriend's family for a weekend getaway and gradually discovers a disturbing secret hidden beneath their seemingly progressive exterior.
explain: Jordan Peele's directorial debut earned him an Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay — the first Black writer to win on a debut film.

title: Spirited Away
plot_clue: A sullen ten-year-old girl finds herself stranded in a fantastical spirit world when her parents are transformed into pigs. To rescue them, she must work in a magical bathhouse.
explain: Studio Ghibli's highest-grossing film — it overtook Titanic in Japan and won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature in 2003.

title: Jurassic Park
plot_clue: A billionaire opens a theme park on a tropical island where genetically cloned prehistoric animals roam. When security systems fail, visitors must survive the escaped predators.
explain: The T. rex roar was a blend of tiger, alligator, and elephant sounds — the iconic footstep vibrations used water in glasses on set.

title: Schindler's List
plot_clue: A German industrialist exploits Jewish labor during World War II but gradually undergoes a moral transformation, eventually spending his entire fortune to save over a thousand Jewish lives.
explain: Shot mostly in black and white, the iconic red coat belonging to a young girl was the only deliberate use of color in the film.

title: Fight Club
plot_clue: A depressed office worker suffering from insomnia befriends a charismatic soap salesman and they form an underground fighting club that evolves into something far more anarchic and dangerous.
explain: The film was a box-office disappointment on release but became a cult classic on DVD — now ranks among the most-discussed films ever made.

title: La La Land
plot_clue: A jazz pianist and an aspiring actress fall in love in Los Angeles, but as their careers take off in different directions, they must choose between their dreams and each other.
explain: At the 2017 Oscars, La La Land was mistakenly announced as Best Picture before Moonlight was correctly declared the winner.

title: Mad Max: Fury Road
plot_clue: In a post-apocalyptic wasteland, a rebel warrior and a drifter join forces to escape a tyrannical warlord across a scorched desert, pursued by his army of war machines.
explain: George Miller shot almost entirely practical stunts in Namibia — approximately 80% of effects were done in camera with minimal CGI.

title: Clueless
plot_clue: A wealthy Beverly Hills teenager who fancies herself a skilled matchmaker attempts to improve the social standing of a nerdy new student while navigating high school politics and her own romantic confusion.
explain: Writer-director Amy Heckerling loosely based the film on Jane Austen's Emma — setting it in a 1990s Beverly Hills high school.

title: The Truman Show
plot_clue: A man living in an idyllic seaside town gradually suspects that his entire life has been an elaborate fabrication staged for television, and that everyone around him is an actor.
explain: The concept of 'Truman Show Delusion' — where people believe their lives are secretly being filmed — is now recognized by psychiatrists.

title: Whiplash
plot_clue: A driven jazz drumming student at an elite conservatory is pushed to his psychological and physical limits by his ruthlessly demanding instructor who believes that great achievement requires great suffering.
explain: J.K. Simmons won the Oscar for Best Supporting Actor and was so physically imposing that Miles Teller's hands bled during filming.

title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
plot_clue: A middle-aged Chinese-American laundromat owner learns she can access skills from parallel universe versions of herself, and discovers she alone can save the multiverse from a nihilistic entity.
choices: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
explain: Swept the 2023 Oscars with 7 wins including Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actress, Best Supporting Actor, and Best Supporting Actress.
